detail:
Real, narrow gap in the FA-0026 fix. The fix added a stale-RUNNING re-dispatch self-heal, but it lives AFTER the limit check: execute_automation calls _check_limits at services.py:401 BEFORE get_or_create at :433. When the limit check returns not-allowed for a SCHEDULE trigger, control goes to the early SKIPPED branch (services.py:406-417) which does a bare AutomationRun.objects.create(...) with the window-deterministic key schedule:<id>:<epoch> (epoch derived from scheduled_for == automation.next_run_at). idempotency_key is unique=True (models.py:70), so if a stranded RUNNING row for that same window already exists, the create raises IntegrityError instead of short-circuiting into the new re-dispatch recovery (which only runs on the created=False path it never reaches). run_due_automations catches it as a generic errors+=1 (scheduler.py:50-53) and continues WITHOUT advancing next_run_at, so the automation is re-selected every tick and crashes forever — the exact unrecoverable, silent failure class the original defect described, now reachable through a different door.

The reviewer's MIN_RUN_INTERVAL trigger is valid and concrete: last_run_at is set (services.py:487) only on a SUCCESS, and only SCHEDULE successes advance next_run_at (services.py:490). A successful MANUAL run (views.py:113, no scheduled_for -> now) sets last_run_at to ~now (later than the stranded window T) WITHOUT advancing next_run_at, leaving it pinned at T. On the next scheduler tick, _check_limits(reference_utc=T) computes (T - last_run_at) as a negative timedelta, which is < 120min, so MIN_RUN_INTERVAL fails -> SKIPPED create -> IntegrityError collision with the stranded row's key.

Two caveats on framing: (1) the SKIPPED-branch create() was non-idempotent BEFORE the fix too, so the collision mechanism is pre-existing, not literally introduced by the fix; what the fix did was claim self-healing for strands while leaving this sub-path uncovered, so it is an INCOMPLETE fix rather than a pure regression. (2) Reachability is strictly rarer than the original FA-0026: it needs the same sub-ms hard-kill strand PLUS a subsequent last_run_at advance (e.g. a manual Run-now) while next_run_at stays pinned, PLUS the stranded row still present and not yet healed by a limit-passing tick. That compound precondition is why low (not medium) is right. The original defect's suggested reaper / advance-next_run_at-in-atomic-block fix would not have this hole.
suggested_fix: Make the limit-check-fail SKIPPED branch idempotent and strand-aware: replace the bare AutomationRun.objects.create at services.py:406 with get_or_create(idempotency_key=...) (defaults = the SKIPPED row) wrapped in transaction.atomic, and when created=False, apply the same stale-RUNNING re-dispatch / terminal-row handling used in the created=False block (or simply still advance next_run_at). Even simpler and more robust per the original suggested_fix: advance next_run_at unconditionally for SCHEDULE triggers inside the same atomic block as run creation (so a strand can never leave next_run_at pinned), and/or add the stale-RUNNING reaper cron. Either eliminates the deterministic-key collision crash loop entirely.
evidence: apps/automations/services.py:401-420 — limit check runs before get_or_create; SKIPPED branch uses bare create() with deterministic key and does not reach the stale-RUNNING recovery
apps/automations/services.py:438-455 — re-dispatch self-heal only on the created=False (get_or_create) path, bypassed when the limit check fails first
apps/automations/services.py:271-275 — MIN_RUN_INTERVAL trips when (reference_utc - last_run_at) < 120min; negative timedelta after a later success qualifies
apps/automations/services.py:487,490 — last_run_at set on any success; only SCHEDULE advances next_run_at, so a manual success pins next_run_at while moving last_run_at forward
apps/automations/views.py:113-116 — manual Run-now path calls execute_automation with no scheduled_for
apps/automations/models.py:70 — idempotency_key unique=True so the duplicate create() raises IntegrityError
apps/automations/scheduler.py:43-53 — exception swallowed as errors+=1, continue, next_run_at not advanced -> automation re-selected and re-crashes every tick
